root_text.an3.79:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.79:2.1: “Tīṇimāni, bhante, gandhajātāni, yesaṁ anuvātaṁyeva gandho gacchati, no paṭivātaṁ.
root_text.an3.79:2.4: imāni kho, bhante, tīṇi gandhajātāni, yesaṁ anuvātaṁyeva gandho gacchati, no paṭivātaṁ.
root_text.an3.79:2.5: Atthi nu kho, bhante, kiñci gandhajātaṁ yassa anuvātampi gandho gacchati, paṭivātampi gandho gacchati, anuvātapaṭivātampi gandho gacchatī”ti?
root_text.an3.79:3.1: “Atthānanda, kiñci gandhajātaṁ yassa anuvātampi gandho gacchati, paṭivātampi gandho gacchati, anuvātapaṭivātampi gandho gacchatī”ti.
root_text.an3.79:3.2: “Katamañca pana, bhante, gandhajātaṁ yassa anuvātampi gandho gacchati, paṭivātampi gandho gacchati, anuvātapaṭivātampi gandho gacchatī”ti?
root_text.an3.79:4.1: “Idhānanda, yasmiṁ gāme vā nigame vā itthī vā puriso vā buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti,
root_text.an3.79:4.2: pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti, sīlavā hoti kalyāṇadhammo,
root_text.an3.79:4.3: vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgo payatapāṇi vossaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato.
root_text.an3.79:5.2: ‘amukasmiṁ nāma gāme vā nigame vā itthī vā puriso vā buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti,
root_text.an3.79:5.3: pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti, sīlavā hoti kalyāṇadhammo,
root_text.an3.79:5.4: vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgo payatapāṇi vossaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato’ti.
root_text.an3.79:6.2: ‘amukasmiṁ nāma gāme vā nigame vā itthī vā puriso vā buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti,
root_text.an3.79:6.3: pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti …pe… surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti, sīlavā hoti kalyāṇadhammo,
root_text.an3.79:6.4: vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgo payatapāṇi vossaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato’ti.
root_text.an3.79:6.5: Idaṁ kho taṁ, ānanda, gandhajātaṁ yassa anuvātampi gandho gacchati, paṭivātampi gandho gacchati, anuvātapaṭivātampi gandho gacchatīti.

translation_text.an3.79:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an3.79:2.1: “Sir, there are these three kinds of fragrance that spread only with the wind, not against it.
translation_text.an3.79:2.3: The fragrance of roots, heartwood, and flowers.
translation_text.an3.79:2.4: These are the three kinds of fragrance that spread only with the wind, not against it.
translation_text.an3.79:2.5: Is there a kind of fragrance that spreads with the wind, and against it, and both ways?”
translation_text.an3.79:3.1: “There is, Ānanda, such a kind of fragrance.”
translation_text.an3.79:3.2: “So what, sir, is that kind of fragrance?”
translation_text.an3.79:4.1: “It’s when, Ānanda, in some village or town, a woman or man has gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an3.79:4.2: They don’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants. They’re ethical, of good character.
translation_text.an3.79:4.3: They live at home with a heart rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.an3.79:5.1: Ascetics and brahmins everywhere praise them for these good qualities;
translation_text.an3.79:6.5: This is the kind of fragrance that spreads with the wind, and against it, and both.
translation_text.an3.79:7.1: The fragrance of flowers <j>doesn’t spread against the wind,
translation_text.an3.79:7.2: nor sandalwood, pinwheel flowers, or jasmine;
translation_text.an3.79:7.3: but the fragrance of the good <j>spreads against the wind;
translation_text.an3.79:7.4: a true person’s virtue spreads in every direction.”

variant_text.an3.79:3.1: Atthānanda, kiñci gandhajātaṁ → atthānanda gandhajātaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.79:6.1: Devatāpissa → devatāpissa amanussā (bj, pts1ed); devatāpissa amanussāpi (mr); devatāpissa …pe… manussāpissa (?)
